stem: A biologist models the population of a bacterial culture in a controlled environment. Each day from day 0 to day 5, the number of bacteria in the culture is 200% more than the number on the previous day. The model estimates that on day 1 there were 240 bacteria. Which of the following equations represents this model, where n is the estimated number of bacteria on day t?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. A 200% increase multiplies the prior value by 1 + 2.00 = 3, so the model has the form n = a(3)t. On day 1, n = 240, so 240 = a(3)1, giving a = 80. The equation is n = 80(3)t.

Choice A uses a 100% increase factor of 2 with the day-1 value as the initial value. Choice B uses the day-1 value as the initial value. Choice C uses a 100% increase factor of 2.
